info.title: WebSharper .NET CLI version
info.description: Dotnet tool to manage WebSharper related projects

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build the WebSharper project in the current folder
(or in the nearest parent folder). You can optionally
specify a project file using `--project`.

options.name: --project
options.description: Specify the project file to build.

commands.name: compile
commands.description:
Compile a given fs(x) file to JavaScript or
TypeScript utilizing WebSharper's Booster if enabled

options.name: --version
options.description: Specify the version of WebSharper to use.

options.name: --path
options.description: Specify the output location for library mode.

options.name: --watch
options.description: Initiates a file watcher for the given fsx file.

options.name: --force
options.description:
Forces the temporary folder to be flushed before the
compilation.

commands.name: info
commands.description: Prints the version of the dotnet ws tool

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build the WebSharper project in the current folder
(or in the nearest parent folder). You can optionally
specify a project file using `--project`.

commands.name: compile
commands.description:
Compile a given fs(x) file to JavaScript or
TypeScript utilizing WebSharper's Booster if enabled

commands.name: list
commands.description: List running Booster versions and their source paths.

commands.name: start
commands.description:
Start the Booster service (wsfscservice) with the
given RID and version. If no value is given for
version, the latest (as found in the local NuGet
cache) will be used.

commands.name: stop
commands.description:
Send a stop signal to the Booster service with the
given version. If no version is given all running
instances are signaled. Use `--force` to kill
process(es) instead of sending a stop signal.

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Sets up a file watcher, that triggers recompilation
via dotnet ws compile or dotnet ws build depending on
the scenario

commands.name: start
commands.description:
Start the Booster service (wsfscservice) with the
given RID and version. If no value is given for
version, the latest (as found in the local NuGet
cache) will be used.

options.name: --rid
options.description:
Specify the Runtime Identifier
(win-x64|linux-x64|linux-musl-x64) to use.

options.name: --version
options.description: Specify the version of the Booster service.

commands.name: stop
commands.description:
Send a stop signal to the Booster service with the
given version. If no version is given all running
instances are signaled. Use `--force` to kill
process(es) instead of sending a stop signal.

options.name: --version
options.description: Specify the version of the Booster service.

options.name: --force
options.description:
Force the specified operation (used for the stop
subcommand).

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Sets up a file watcher, that triggers recompilation
via dotnet ws compile or dotnet ws build depending on
the scenario
